nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and seaward reefs over rubble and sand bottoms, from shallow silty estuaries and inlets out to outer reef slopes; typically 1–10 m depth (recorded down to about 50 m). Adults occupy burrows (which they do not generally excavate themselves) and hover just above the substrate feeding on current-borne zooplankton, usually in monogamous pairs or small groups.

decorPreferences: open sand bed at least 5 cm deep for burrowing or hiding
loose coral rubble piled to form hiding crevices
live rock with small caves and overhangs
open swimming space above the substrate

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on current-borne zooplankton hovering near its burrow. In captivity generally accepts mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, copepods, finely chopped krill or table shrimp, and frozen carnivore preparations. Multiple small feedings per day (commonly suggested 2–3 or more) are generally advised, particularly during acclimation when individuals tend to be shy.

summary: The Green Silk Gudgeon Dartfish is a peaceful, reef-safe Indo-Pacific dartfish generally considered beginner-friendly. It tends to hover just above the sand bed near burrows it shares with a mate and is known to be a jumper, so a tightly sealed lid is generally advised.
careNotes:
Generally advised to keep in an established reef or fish-only-with-live-rock aquarium with a sandy substrate (commonly suggested 5+ cm deep), loose coral rubble piled to form hiding crevices, and open swimming space above the sand bed. This species has been reported to occupy burrows but generally does not excavate them itself, so providing pre-formed hiding spaces (rubble pockets, small caves) is commonly suggested. It tends to be very shy after introduction; multiple small feedings per day and dimmed lighting during the first days have been reported to help draw it out.

Widely regarded as a jumper — a tight-fitting lid with no gaps is generally considered essential. Escape risk is high, consistent with most dartfish in the hobby.

Generally considered peaceful and docile toward all tank mates, including conspecifics, and has been reported to do best singly or as a male-female pair. In the wild, adults are commonly reported to form monogamous pairs sharing a burrow. Best avoided with overtly aggressive or predatory tankmates (groupers, dottybacks, triggerfish, aggressive angelfishes, lionfish).

Source spread: minimum-tank recommendations ranged from 30 US gal (~114 L, LiveAquaria) to 100 L (Reefapp.net) up to 50 US gal (~189 L, BlueZoo Aquatics). The value used here (150 L, ≈ 40 US gal) biases toward the larger recommendations to reflect the species' need for open swimming space and the recommendation to keep as a pair. Max size: 5 in (≈12.7 cm, LiveAquaria and BlueZoo) and 13 cm TL (FishBase, Reefapp); the range used here (12–13 cm) averages across these. Temperature: 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) at LiveAquaria, and 22–27°C at FishBase wild data; the range here (22–27°C) takes the broader FishBase wild envelope.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been well documented in commonly available hobbyist literature. In the wild, adults are commonly reported to form monogamous pairs sharing a burrow, but specific aquarium spawning protocols are not established.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection page and species product page (/products/green-silk-gudgeon-dartfish) provided scientific name, max size (5 in), minimum tank (30 US gal / ~114 L), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), care level (easy/beginner), temperament (peaceful), reef-safe (yes), diet (carnivore/planktivore), family (Microdesmidae), and husbandry notes (jumper, burrowing, multiple feedings).

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, common name (Green Gudgeon Goby), max size (5 in), minimum tank (50 US gal / ~189 L), care level (beginner), temperament (peaceful), reef-safe (yes), diet (planktivore), and origin (Indo-Pacific, Red Sea, Australia).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Authoritative taxonomy (Gobiiformes / Microdesmidae), max published length 13.0 cm TL, depth 1–50 m (typically to ~10 m), Indo-Pacific distribution (Red Sea through Line/Tuamoto Islands, north to Ryukyu Islands, south to southern Great Barrier Reef), wild temperature 22–27°C, monogamous mating, zooplankton diet.

additional.name: Reefapp.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (13 cm), minimum tank (100 L), temperament (very shy/docile), reef-safe (always), diet (small crustaceans, zooplankton), pair-keeping, jumping risk, and the note that the species does not excavate its own burrow.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding protocols are not established in commonly available hobbyist sources. In the wild, adults are commonly reported to form monogamous pairs sharing a burrow, but specific aquarium spawning has not been well documented.
